title: jacobian OVER a forward derivative that captures the outer vector via vector-ref returns 0 (jacobian analogue of #113/ESH-0117, fails even at depth 1)
goal: Found by the depth-parametric AD oracle (P6a), verified on -r AND AOT (identical). The reverse-over-forward fix (#113) makes a GRADIENT (scalar) outer over a forward derivative that closes over the outer vector work, but the JACOBIAN (vector-field) outer of the SAME construction returns 0 — the inner forward-derivative's dependence on the outer vector is erased when the outer op is jacobian. Fails already at nesting depth 1. Minimal repro (tests/ad_depth/found/esh_0120_jacobian_over_forward_zero.esk): grad-outer = (vref (gradient (lambda(v)(derivative (lambda(z)(* (vref v 0) z z z z z z z z)) 2.0)) (vector 3.0 4.0)) 0) = 1024 CORRECT; jac-outer = J[0][0] of (jacobian (lambda(v)(vector (derivative (lambda(z)(* (vref v 0) z^8) 2.0)) (derivative (lambda(z)(* (vref v 1) z^8) 2.0)))) (vector 3.0 4.0)) = 0 WRONG (expected 1024). Likely the same mixed-record mechanism as #113 needs wiring for the jacobian's per-output-row reverse seeds.
detection_gap: P3 oracle's jacobian cells never composed jacobian with an inner AD op. P6a jacod.* cells (scripts/gen_ad_depth.py) sweep jacobian-over-derivative^d; tracked by the ad-depth gate baseline (max-correct-depth 0).
acceptance: jacobian-over-single-forward with vecref returns diag(1024) (-r AND AOT); a 2-vector case matches finite-difference; oracle jacod.* cells reach max-correct-depth >= 1; gradient-outer path unchanged; no regression to ad_oracle / ESH-0117 fix
